item.title: Airlines weigh risks in Mideast skies filled with 'hot pieces of flying metal'
item.description: Confusing advice and on-again-off-again airport closures are complicating the evacuation of hundreds of thousands of foreigners stranded in the conflict zone around the Persian Gulf. In a region that has seen tragic mistakes resulting in the deaths of hundreds of civilian air passengers, decisions on whether to fly remain fairly ad hoc and can come down to the judgment of an individual pilot.

item.title: Why parents are ‘livid’ over this TDSB survey about how to spend money
Frank Stronach’s defence asks for ‘directed verdicts’ on two charges as Crown closes its sex assault case
The little we know about Canadian troops stationed in the Middle East
item.description: OTTAWA - As Ottawa refuses to say exactly how many Canadian troops are in the Middle East, experts are questioning the government's claim it h…

item.title: Canada hoping to charter flights for Canadians to leave U.A.E. in next 72 hours: Anand
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand says the government is looking at getting Canadians out of the Middle East, including asking the United Arab Emirates to use its airspace for chartered evacuation flights.

item.title: OpenAI CEO expressed 'horror and responsibility' over ChatGPT's ties to Tumbler Ridge, AI minister says
item.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon says OpenAI's CEO has agreed to let Canadian experts into its safety office to help evaluate future threats following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C.

item.title: Ottawa arranges charter, commercial flights as more than 2,000 Canadians ask for help leaving Middle East
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand said she expects flights to leave for UAE in the next 72 hours

item.title: Carney won’t ‘rule out’ Canadian military involvement in Middle East
item.description: He suggested this would happen only under extraordinary circumstances and gave no indication any operations are under development

item.title: Carney tells Australian Parliament allies must draw closer as global order is 'breaking down'
item.description: In an address to Australia's Parliament that was something of a love letter to the country and its people, Prime Minister Mark Carney implored citizens in both Commonwealth realms to draw closer together amid a breakdown in the established global order.

item.title: ‘We’re not okay’: Calls for more support as Ontarians with intellectual disabilities face homelessness and exploitation
item.description: More Ontarians with intellectual and developmental disabilities are losing their homes and facing exploitation, creating, what advocates are calling, a system in crisis.

item.title: Michael Ford says he’s giving ‘serious consideration’ to Toronto mayoral run
item.description: Video

item.title: Ontario government to bring back paper report cards
Alberta 'excessively vulnerable' to foreign interference, experts warn
item.description: Alberta is not ready to deal with the threat of online disinformation coming from foreign actors in a possible referendum campaign on separation this fall, according to national security experts.

item.title: Arrests made at Toronto demonstration against changes to OSAP
item.description: Toronto police have arrested several people after a protest at Queen’s Park got heated Wednesday afternoon

item.title: Canada’s AI minister says OpenAI to change ChatGPT after Tumbler Ridge shooting
Mark Carney refuses to ‘categorically’ rule out military involvement in Middle East conflict
Three charged, more than $2.3 million in suspected fentanyl seized in interprovincial police probe
How John Tory’s exit, some bad blood — and a Ford — are threatening to shake up Toronto’s mayoral race
item.description: As Brad Bradford moves closer to solidifying himself as the consensus centre-right mayoral challenger to progressive incumbent Olivia Chow, he…

item.title: Canadians want floor-crossing MPs to face ‘immediate’ byelections: poll
item.description: The Ipsos poll conducted for Global News found Canadians' displeasure with floor-crossing MPs has not hurt the overall approval for Prime Minister Mark Carney or the Liberals.

item.title: Mark Carney’s Iran reaction raises doubts about what he stands for
Carney government working to bring 2,035 Canadians home from the Middle East, Anita Anand says
Rally over OSAP changes gets heated as protesters clash with police at Queen’s Park
Carney shakes up senior ranks of the public service
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has shaken up the senior ranks of the public service, creating and expanding some roles in a continued effort to put his stamp on the machinery of government.

item.title: Moroun, Ambassador Bridge company turned to ex-Harper aides’ firm to lobby Canadian officials
item.description: The billionaire U.S. owners of the busiest land border crossing in North America turned to a high-powered lobbying firm run by former top aides to Prime Minister Stephen Harper in recent years, records show.

item.title: Michael Ford, premier’s nephew, considering run for Toronto mayor
Mark Carney backs U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran despite his doubts over their legality
How Canada’s position on the Iran war has evolved throughout the week
Carney supports U.S. strikes on Iran ‘with regret’ — what are other leaders saying?
item.description: OTTAWA - Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday Canada supports U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran "with regret" — making him just one among …

item.title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman to meet AI minister, B.C. premier over safety changes
item.description: Evan Solomon sought the meeting after OpenAI said it would enhance its police referral threshold and other safety measures after fallout over the Tumbler Ridge mass shooting.

item.title: Foreign Affairs Minister Anand says NATO must turn its focus to the North
NATO must look ‘northward’ to Arctic threats, Anand warns in speech
item.description: Last month, Canada opened a consulate in Nuuk, Greenland, as U.S. President Donald Trump escalated his threats to acquire the semiautonomous territory of NATO ally Denmark.

item.title: Doug Ford’s interest in Exhibition Place for new conference centre blindsides Toronto officials
item.description: Exhibition Place officials had been developing a plan for the future of the site when the premier's comments caught them off-guard.

item.title: Toronto to look at paying people to shovel sidewalks — just like New York City does
item.description: The city has struggled with its sidewalk plows, which have been notoriously unreliable.

item.title: Ottawa shuffles senior public service ranks
item.description: Announcement names 16 individuals who will be taking on new roles

item.title: John Tory decides not to seek re-election as Toronto’s mayor because of ‘inevitable attacks’
Anand says Ottawa working to help Canadians stranded in Middle East
item.description: There are no immediate plans to send military planes to the region to bring Canadians home, Foreign Affairs Minister says

item.title: Former Toronto mayor John Tory announces he won’t run in upcoming election
item.description: Former Toronto mayor John Tory says he will not seek the city’s top job in the fall municipal election.

item.title: Nova Scotia, Ontario alcohol deal garnering mixed reviews
item.description: A deal by Nova Scotia and Ontario to allow consumers to buy craft beer, wine and other alcoholic beverages directly from producers in each province is garnering mixed reviews.

item.title: Mother urges Canada to swiftly repatriate her son after reported transfer to Iraq
item.description: Canadian mother says she fears for son's safety behind bars in Iraq, receiving word he may have been transferred there from detention in north…

item.title: Airlines suspend flights to Middle East as conflict between Iran and U.S., Israel intensifies
Why pumping more Canadian crude won’t stop oil prices from soaring
item.description: The postWhy pumping more Canadian crude won’t stop oil prices from soaringappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Federal government boosts scrutiny of businesses claiming to be Indigenous-owned
item.description: Gull-Masty stopped short of accepting the committee’s recommendation to put in place new eligibility and verification requirements for companies to be listed as Indigenous.

item.title: Critical minerals alliance with Australia an example of middle power strength, Carney says
item.description: Countries such as Canada and Australia have more power than they realize, especially in critical minerals, Prime Minister said in an address to Australia’s Lowy Institute

item.title: Immigration Department experimenting with AI to advise newcomers on where to settle
item.description: IRCC also using AI to detect fraud, including fake documents and morphed photographs

item.title: How Doug Ford got pulled into a fight over a Toronto hockey arena and youth centre — and why this faceoff may be ending
U.S. could hold a 'red card' over Canada's Gripen fighter jet option
item.description: As Ottawa reviews its F-35 purchase, there's concern Washington holds a powerful lever — control of the secure Link 16 network used for NATO and NORAD operations. Swedish Gripens and Danish F-35s operate together in NATO missions, but U.S. approval is required for the encryption system — raising concerns it could be withheld if Canada chooses the Gripen.

item.title: Saab sees Canada as 'great' partner to design next-generation fighter jets
item.description: Swedish company Saab says it's hoping to partner with the federal government and Canadian businesses to develop systems for the next generation of aerial warfare as it pursues deals to supply Ottawa with new aircraft.

item.title: Mark Carney cites ‘progress’ after ‘frank discussions’ on India’s alleged violent crime, interference in Canada
'It's not easy, let's be clear': Carney speaks frankly about dealing with Trump at an event in Australia
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney pulled back the curtain on his relationship with his American counterpart on Wednesday evening in Australia, saying it's not easy dealing with U.S. President Donald Trump and he must choose his words carefully to avoid any blowback.

item.title: Carney won't say if there's Indian interference in Canada after cutting deals with Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney wouldn't take a stand Wednesday on whether he thinks Indian agents are actively interfering in Canada's affairs, saying there's an ongoing criminal matter related to the murder of a B.C. Sikh man and he doesn't want to prejudice the outcome of that case.

item.title: Commandant of Canadian Army Command and Staff College removed from post
NDP politicians set off on road trip to highlight northern Ontario road dangers
item.description: Three Ontario New Democrats have set off on a road trip from Toronto to the Manitoba border and back to highlight the dangers of northern roads.

item.title: Canadians in Persian Gulf say they are living by the grace of missile defence systems
item.description: Canadian expats and those trying to get back home from the Persian Gulf say they're living under the constant threat of missile and drone strikes but are taking solace in what they describe as highly effective air-defence systems keeping them safe from harm.

item.title: Carney says he raised foreign interference concerns in ‘frank’ talk with Modi
item.description: Prime Minister declines to say whether conversation included India’s alleged role in Nijjar’s death

item.title: Carney says his support for U.S. and Israel's war on Iran 'not a blank cheque'
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his support for U.S President Donald Trump's attack on Iran comes amid a "failure of the international order" and is "not a blank cheque."

item.title: Carney says Ottawa’s position supporting U.S., Israeli strikes on Iran was taken ‘with regret’
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have acted without engaging the UN or allies, leaving the question of where things will go from here, Prime Minister says

item.title: Carney reaffirms Canada’s support for strikes on Iran ‘with regret’
item.description: Carney said in Australia that the growing Middle East conflict 'is another example of the failure of the international order' and that Canada's support 'is not a blank cheque.'

item.title: John Tory says he won’t run for mayor of Toronto
item.description: The former mayor resigned in 2023 after admitting to an extramarital affair

item.title: Why most Canadians will keep daylight saving time changes as B.C. stops
item.description: With B.C.'s announcement to adopt a permanent year-round daylight saving time, conversation has now turned to why other provinces have yet to make the change.

item.title: Poilievre touts 'free trade' between 'free nations' in speech on first foreign trip
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre touted the importance of "free trade" between "free nations" in a speech that referenced the likes of Adam Smith, Winston Churchill and even the mythic Robin Hood.

item.title: Ottawa asks Oman to use airspace to help evacuate Canadians from Middle East if necessary
item.description: Anand says the government’s top priority is helping the 100,000 citizens stranded in the region

item.title: John Tory in his own words: ‘What I’m asking for is your understanding’
item.description: John Tory's statement announcing his decision not to run for mayor in October.

item.title: NDP politicians set off on road trip to highlight northern Ontario road dangers
Canadian troops in Middle East ‘are all fine’ amid Iran war, McGuinty says
item.description: McGuinty did not offer specifics Tuesday when asked how many Canadian military personnel are in the region, saying only there are 'some.'

item.title: Carney in Australia to build on ‘important’ trade, defence relationship
item.description: The prime minister arrived in Sydney midday Tuesday local time to meet with business leaders, before heading to Canberra to address the Australian Parliament on Thursday.

item.title: Toronto’s mayor wants to finally fix this foul-smelling city eyesore. It’s unclear how much it will cost
Toronto’s mayor promised to help these tenants. Months later, conditions are so bad Canada Post won’t deliver their mail
Carney lands in Australia looking to draw closer to a Commonwealth cousin also grappling with Trump
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has landed in Australia as he looks to bolster ties with a Commonwealth cousin that is increasingly viewed in Ottawa as a key ally on global challenges — a country that has some newfound importance for Canada amid a lot of uncertainty elsewhere.

item.title: Nervous nations calling Canada's energy minister after Iran strikes
item.description: Energy Minister Tim Hodgson says he's starting to get calls from countries about how Canadian energy producers can fill the gap amid the war in Iran, which is driving up oil and gas prices.​

item.title: Iran’s former leader was ‘force for evil,’ Defence Minister says, as he defends Ottawa’s backing of air strikes
item.description: Responding to critics of government’s stand on U.S.-Israel attacks, David McGuinty says party is a big tent with broad range of views

item.title: No time for truth in this reset with India
item.description: Mark Carney keeps away from reporters in India to avoid questions about foreign interference

item.title: Poilievre lands in London for first foreign trip as Opposition leader
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is on a European tour that is taking him to the United Kingdom and Germany where he is meeting with like-minded politicians and laying out a political and economic vision he hopes can rival Prime Minister Mark Carney.

item.title: Did Carney go too far in offering 'support' for U.S. strikes against Iran?
item.description: Every prime minister is called upon, at one time or another, to comment on the actions of an American president. For Mark Carney, the latest moment of necessity arrived this past weekend — when the United States and Israel launched new attacks on Iran.

item.title: No more federal budget watchdog in Ottawa as interim PBO’s term expires
item.description: Interim PBO Jason Jacques was appointed to a six-month term in September that ended at 5 p.m. ET Monday without a successor in place.

item.title: Ontario teacher, education worker unions call for contract talks to start early
item.description: Contracts for Ontario’s teachers and education workers expire at the end of this summer and their union leaders are calling on the education minister to start the bargaining process early, saying issues such as class sizes need to be urgently addressed.

item.title: Ring of Fire roads to be complete by 2031, ahead of schedule: Ford
item.description: Construction on the roads to the Ring of Fire in northern Ontario should start this year and be completed by 2031, the province announced Monday about a project that has divided several First Nations.

item.title: Doug Ford names new chief of staff with experience in Canada-U.S. relations
item.description: Travis Kann currently holds the dual role of principal secretary and head of Ontario-U.S. relations

item.title: Danielle Smith says Iran war underscores need for new Alberta pipeline to the coast
item.description: Danielle Smith says any disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil choke point at the mouth of the Persian Gulf, underscores the need for a new Alberta pipeline to the Pacific.

item.title: Politics Insider: Carney secures uranium supply deal with India
item.description: The Prime Minister, who has set a goal of doubling non-U.S. trade over the next decade, said co-operation with India would make both countries more self-reliant

item.title: Ontario and Nova Scotia to allow direct-to-consumer alcohol sales between provinces
item.description: Ontario and Nova Scotia will begin allowing consumers to buy alcohol directly from producers in each province as both jurisdictions look for ways to increase internal trade within Canada in the face of the U.S. trade war.

item.title: From shipping chaos to spiking oil prices, here’s how war in the Middle East is affecting Canadian business
item.description: The postFrom shipping chaos to spiking oil prices, here’s how war in the Middle East is affecting Canadian businessappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Plans in works to replace Metro Toronto Convention Centre, Ford says
item.description: Toronto could soon be getting a modern new convention centre to replace its marquee convention space, Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Tuesday.

item.title: Ontario teachers and Ford government are at odds — over when to begin contract talks
item.description: Ontario's teacher and education unions are calling on the Ford government to start as early as this month. The province isn't biting.

item.title: Ontario and Nova Scotia reach direct-to-consumer booze deal
Ottawa weighs splitting submarine contract between Europe and South Korea, sources say
item.description: Final proposals were received on Monday, and the government is set to make a procurement decision by April 4 at earliest

item.title: What’s next for the sprawling ravine site of the shuttered Ontario Science Centre?
The number of Ontarians over 80 is about to surge. Advocates say the Ford government needs a strategy
Ottawa boosting support, but warns Canadians in Middle East to prepare contingency plans
item.description: Global Affairs says government’s ability to provide consular services during the conflict is limited
